hero.title: MTG Price Checker App

sections.title: The best MTG Price Checker App

items.title: Quick card price checks

items.title: Price context inside your collection

items.title: Better buy, sell, and trade decisions

sections.title: Check MTG prices with more context

sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.description: Helpful answers about using Spellvault as your MTG Price Checker App.

items.title: What is an MTG price checker?
items.description: An MTG price checker helps you review card values quickly, so you can make better buying, selling, trading, and collection decisions.

items.title: Why use Spellvault instead of a simple price site?
items.description: Spellvault keeps pricing connected to your real collection, which means you can check value while still seeing duplicates, locations, and your own copies.

items.title: Can I check prices for cards I already own?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault is especially useful when you want to check value inside your collection workflow, not just look at a single card in isolation.

items.title: Does Spellvault help with buy, sell, and trade decisions?
items.description: Yes. Price checks become more actionable when you can compare the value of a card with how many copies you own and how you use them.

items.title: Can I see duplicate copies while checking MTG prices?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault helps you understand price together with duplicates, which is useful when you are deciding what to sell, trade, or keep.

items.title: Is Spellvault useful for MTG sellers and stores?
items.description: Yes. Stores, sellers, and active traders all benefit from checking price with more inventory context and less manual lookup work.

items.title: Can I use Spellvault as both a price checker and collection tracker?
items.description: Yes. Spellvault is designed to connect pricing with collection management, so you do not need one app for prices and another for inventory.

items.title: Does Spellvault only check single card prices?
items.description: No. You can use Spellvault for single card price checks, but it is also useful for understanding price across the rest of your collection.
